what_it_does: Defines a 24 by 24 SVG icon that draws a right-pointing arrow using a single stroked path.
observations: Uses an inline SVG element with xmlns and viewBox attributes.
Sets fill to none and stroke to currentColor.
Uses stroke width 1.5 with round line caps and joins.
Draws a horizontal line from left to right and angled segments forming an arrowhead.
Contains one path element and no embedded text, metadata, title, or script.
standouts: The icon is stroke-only and minimal.
The arrow shaft and arrowhead are encoded in one path.
conventions: Follows common line-icon conventions with no fill, rounded caps, and rounded joins.
concerns: No title, desc, role, or aria attributes are present in the SVG itself.
notes: A compact reusable right-arrow SVG icon intended to inherit color from its rendering context.
questions: Should accessibility labeling be supplied by the consumer or embedded in this asset?
Is the 1.5 stroke width the intended icon stroke standard for this set?
purpose_vs_actual: The path and filename indicate a right-arrow icon, and the file draws a right-pointing arrow.
intention_signals: It seems intended to provide a simple rightward navigation or continuation glyph that adapts to surrounding text or UI color.
contribution: Provides a reusable, color-inheriting right-arrow glyph with a 24x24 coordinate system and rounded stroke treatment.
summary_for_embedding: This is a compact SVG asset defining a right-pointing arrow icon with currentColor stroke styling in a 24x24 viewBox.
intention_for_embedding: This was for representing forward, next, or rightward direction in a UI while inheriting surrounding color.
